toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * EL0 stubs for slice 2.5 ping-pong demo.
 *
 * Two PC-relative-free blobs, each copied into its own physical page by
 * `userland_bootstrap` (mapped EL0 RX at USER_CODE_VA_{A,B}). The stubs
 * exercise all three sync IPC primitives:
 *
 *   Stub A — sender: SENDREC to endpoint B in a loop, incrementing a
 *   counter held in x19 (AAPCS callee-saved; the SVC entry/exit code in
 *   trap.S saves and restores all of x0..x30, so x19 survives the trap).
 *
 *   Stub B — echo server: RECEIVE from ANY, then SEND back to whoever
 *   m_source identifies. Mirrors m_type unchanged.
 *
 *   Stub C — kernel-call client (slice 2.6): SENDREC to the SYSTEM
 *   endpoint with m_type = SYS_GETINFO and payload[0..4] = GET_WHOAMI in
 *   a loop. The kernel dispatcher writes endpoint, priv-flags, init-flags,
 *   and name back into the payload; the reply's m_type is OK (0).
 *
 *   Stub D — page-fault demo (slice 3.4b): touches an unmapped heap page at
 *   0x0100_0000 in a loop. The first store faults; the kernel routes a
 *   VM_PAGEFAULT to the user-space VM server, which maps the page via
 *   SYS_VMCTL and clears D's fault, after which the store retries and
 *   succeeds. Later iterations hit the now-mapped page with no further
 *   faults. D issues no SVC of its own.
 *
 * Each stub carves its 104-byte Message buffer out of [SP_EL0 - 104,
 * SP_EL0). SP stays 16-byte aligned (104 rounds to nothing modulo 16;
 * we never sub more than 104 so the stack top remains usable). The stack
 * pages are 4 KiB so we have headroom.
 *
 * Endpoint constants below are the boot endpoints of slots 11, 12, and
 * the SYSTEM kernel task (-2). make_endpoint(0, p) = p & 0x7FFF, so
 * SYSTEM at gen 0 = (-2 & 0x7FFF) = 0x7FFE. ANY's encoding is
 * `(1 << 14) - 1 = 0x3FFF` (ENDPOINT_SLOT_TOP at generation 0).
 */

    .equ SEND,         1
    .equ RECEIVE,      2
    .equ SENDREC,      3
    .equ ANY,          0x3FFF
    .equ ENDPOINT_A,   11
    .equ ENDPOINT_B,   12
    .equ ENDPOINT_SYS, 0x7FFE              // boot_endpoint(SYSTEM)
    .equ SYS_GETINFO,  0x600               // KERNEL_CALL + 0
    .equ GET_WHOAMI,   12
    .equ MSG_SIZE,     104                 // sizeof(Message); fits in `mov` imm12

/* ---- Stub A: sender ----------------------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_a, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_a_start
    .type _user_stub_a_start, %object
_user_stub_a_start:
    mov     x19, #0                         // persistent message counter
1:
    // Build Message at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0): m_source=0, m_type=counter.
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]                   // m_source — kernel overwrites
    str     w19, [x9, #4]                   // m_type   — counter
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_B
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_a_end
_user_stub_a_end:
    .size _user_stub_a_start, _user_stub_a_end - _user_stub_a_start

/* ---- Stub B: echo server ----------------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_b, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_b_start
    .type _user_stub_b_start, %object
_user_stub_b_start:
1:
    // Build Message at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0). Kernel fills it on RECEIVE.
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    mov     x0, #ANY
    mov     x1, #RECEIVE
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    // Reply: read m_source out of the delivered message, SEND back with
    // the same m_type already in [x9 + 4].
    ldr     w10, [x9, #0]                   // m_source (signed Endpoint)
    sxtw    x10, w10
    mov     x0, x10
    mov     x1, #SEND
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_b_end
_user_stub_b_end:
    .size _user_stub_b_start, _user_stub_b_end - _user_stub_b_start

/* ---- Stub C: kernel-call client --------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_c, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_c_start
    .type _user_stub_c_start, %object
_user_stub_c_start:
    mov     x19, #0                         // persistent call counter
1:
    // Build the request at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0):
    //   [+0]   m_source — kernel overwrites
    //   [+4]   m_type    = SYS_GETINFO
    //   [+8]   payload[0..4] = GET_WHOAMI
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]
    mov     w10, #SYS_GETINFO
    str     w10, [x9, #4]
    mov     w10, #GET_WHOAMI
    str     w10, [x9, #8]
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_SYS
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_c_end
_user_stub_c_end:
    .size _user_stub_c_start, _user_stub_c_end - _user_stub_c_start

/* ---- Stub D: page-fault demo (slice 3.4b) ------------------------------ */

/*
 * Stub D touches an unmapped heap page at 0x0100_0000 in a loop. The first
 * store faults (translation); the kernel records the fault, blocks D on
 * RTS_PAGEFAULT, and sends VM_PAGEFAULT to the user-space VM server. VM maps
 * the page (SYS_VMCTL(VMCTL_PT_MAP)) and clears the fault
 * (VMCTL_CLEAR_PAGEFAULT); D's store then retries and succeeds. Every later
 * iteration writes the now-mapped page with no fault. D issues no SVC.
 */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_d, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_d_start
    .type _user_stub_d_start, %object
_user_stub_d_start:
    movz    x20, #0x0100, lsl #16              // x20 = 0x0100_0000 (heap VA)
    mov     x19, #0                            // persistent counter
1:
    str     x19, [x20]                         // 1st iter faults → kernel→VM maps it
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_d_end
_user_stub_d_end:
    .size _user_stub_d_start, _user_stub_d_end - _user_stub_d_start

input.old_string:
/* ---- Stub D: page-fault demo (slice 3.4b) ------------------------------ */

/*
 * Stub D touches an unmapped heap page at 0x0100_0000 in a loop. The first
 * store faults (translation); the kernel records the fault, blocks D on
 * RTS_PAGEFAULT, and sends VM_PAGEFAULT to the user-space VM server. VM maps
 * the page (SYS_VMCTL(VMCTL_PT_MAP)) and clears the fault
 * (VMCTL_CLEAR_PAGEFAULT); D's store then retries and succeeds. Every later
 * iteration writes the now-mapped page with no fault. D issues no SVC.
 */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_d, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_d_start
    .type _user_stub_d_start, %object
_user_stub_d_start:
    movz    x20, #0x0100, lsl #16              // x20 = 0x0100_0000 (heap VA)
    mov     x19, #0                            // persistent counter
1:
    str     x19, [x20]                         // 1st iter faults → kernel→VM maps it
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_d_end
_user_stub_d_end:
    .size _user_stub_d_start, _user_stub_d_end - _user_stub_d_start
input.new_string:
/* ---- Stub D: brk + on-demand paging demo (slice 3.5) ------------------- */

/*
 * Stub D drives the VM server's region tracking + brk:
 *
 *   1. VM_BRK(0x0100_4000): SENDREC to VM. VM creates D's heap region
 *      [0x0100_0000, 0x0100_4000) and replies. No frames are mapped yet.
 *   2. Touch heap page 0 (0x0100_0000): translation fault → kernel blocks D on
 *      RTS_PAGEFAULT and sends VM_PAGEFAULT → VM sees the addr is in D's heap,
 *      maps a page (SYS_VMCTL(VMCTL_PT_MAP)) and clears the fault. D retries
 *      and the store succeeds.
 *   3. VM_BRK(0x0100_8000): grow the heap region to [0x0100_0000, 0x0100_8000).
 *   4. Touch heap page 1 (0x0100_4000): only in range *after* step 3 — faults
 *      once, VM maps it. Had step 3 not grown the heap, this would be an
 *      out-of-region SIGSEGV (VM leaves the faulter blocked).
 *   5. Loop writing both pages, incrementing a counter in x19. No further
 *      faults — the pages stay mapped, so D round-robins like A/B/C.
 *
 * x19/x20/x21 are AAPCS callee-saved and survive the SVC (trap.S saves and
 * restores x0..x30). The 104-byte Message is carved from [SP_EL0-104, SP_EL0).
 */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_d, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_d_start
    .type _user_stub_d_start, %object
_user_stub_d_start:
    // --- brk #1: grow heap to 0x0100_4000 ---
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]                     // m_source — kernel overwrites
    mov     w10, #VM_BRK
    str     w10, [x9, #4]                     // m_type = VM_BRK
    movz    x11, #0x0100, lsl #16
    movk    x11, #0x4000
    str     x11, [x9, #8]                     // payload[0..8] = new_break
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_VM
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0

    // --- touch heap page 0 (now in range): faults once, VM maps it ---
    movz    x20, #0x0100, lsl #16             // x20 = 0x0100_0000 (heap page 0)
    mov     x19, #0                           // persistent counter
    str     x19, [x20]

    // --- brk #2: grow heap to 0x0100_8000 ---
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]
    mov     w10, #VM_BRK
    str     w10, [x9, #4]
    movz    x11, #0x0100, lsl #16
    movk    x11, #0x8000
    str     x11, [x9, #8]                     // new_break = 0x0100_8000
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_VM
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0

    // --- touch heap page 1 (in range only after brk #2) ---
    movz    x21, #0x0100, lsl #16
    movk    x21, #0x4000                      // x21 = 0x0100_4000 (heap page 1)
    str     x19, [x21]

    // --- steady loop: write both pages, no further faults ---
1:
    str     x19, [x20]
    str     x19, [x21]
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_d_end
_user_stub_d_end:
    .size _user_stub_d_start, _user_stub_d_end - _user_stub_d_start

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * EL0 stubs for slice 2.5 ping-pong demo.
 *
 * Two PC-relative-free blobs, each copied into its own physical page by
 * `userland_bootstrap` (mapped EL0 RX at USER_CODE_VA_{A,B}). The stubs
 * exercise all three sync IPC primitives:
 *
 *   Stub A — sender: SENDREC to endpoint B in a loop, incrementing a
 *   counter held in x19 (AAPCS callee-saved; the SVC entry/exit code in
 *   trap.S saves and restores all of x0..x30, so x19 survives the trap).
 *
 *   Stub B — echo server: RECEIVE from ANY, then SEND back to whoever
 *   m_source identifies. Mirrors m_type unchanged.
 *
 *   Stub C — kernel-call client (slice 2.6): SENDREC to the SYSTEM
 *   endpoint with m_type = SYS_GETINFO and payload[0..4] = GET_WHOAMI in
 *   a loop. The kernel dispatcher writes endpoint, priv-flags, init-flags,
 *   and name back into the payload; the reply's m_type is OK (0).
 *
 *   Stub D — page-fault demo (slice 3.4b): touches an unmapped heap page at
 *   0x0100_0000 in a loop. The first store faults; the kernel routes a
 *   VM_PAGEFAULT to the user-space VM server, which maps the page via
 *   SYS_VMCTL and clears D's fault, after which the store retries and
 *   succeeds. Later iterations hit the now-mapped page with no further
 *   faults. D issues no SVC of its own.
 *
 * Each stub carves its 104-byte Message buffer out of [SP_EL0 - 104,
 * SP_EL0). SP stays 16-byte aligned (104 rounds to nothing modulo 16;
 * we never sub more than 104 so the stack top remains usable). The stack
 * pages are 4 KiB so we have headroom.
 *
 * Endpoint constants below are the boot endpoints of slots 11, 12, and
 * the SYSTEM kernel task (-2). make_endpoint(0, p) = p & 0x7FFF, so
 * SYSTEM at gen 0 = (-2 & 0x7FFF) = 0x7FFE. ANY's encoding is
 * `(1 << 14) - 1 = 0x3FFF` (ENDPOINT_SLOT_TOP at generation 0).
 */

    .equ SEND,         1
    .equ RECEIVE,      2
    .equ SENDREC,      3
    .equ ANY,          0x3FFF
    .equ ENDPOINT_A,   11
    .equ ENDPOINT_B,   12
    .equ ENDPOINT_VM,  7                   // boot_endpoint(VM_PROC_NR = 7)
    .equ ENDPOINT_SYS, 0x7FFE              // boot_endpoint(SYSTEM)
    .equ SYS_GETINFO,  0x600               // KERNEL_CALL + 0
    .equ GET_WHOAMI,   12
    .equ VM_BRK,       0xC01               // VM_RQ_BASE + 1
    .equ MSG_SIZE,     104                 // sizeof(Message); fits in `mov` imm12

/* ---- Stub A: sender ----------------------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_a, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_a_start
    .type _user_stub_a_start, %object
_user_stub_a_start:
    mov     x19, #0                         // persistent message counter
1:
    // Build Message at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0): m_source=0, m_type=counter.
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]                   // m_source — kernel overwrites
    str     w19, [x9, #4]                   // m_type   — counter
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_B
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_a_end
_user_stub_a_end:
    .size _user_stub_a_start, _user_stub_a_end - _user_stub_a_start

/* ---- Stub B: echo server ----------------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_b, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_b_start
    .type _user_stub_b_start, %object
_user_stub_b_start:
1:
    // Build Message at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0). Kernel fills it on RECEIVE.
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    mov     x0, #ANY
    mov     x1, #RECEIVE
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    // Reply: read m_source out of the delivered message, SEND back with
    // the same m_type already in [x9 + 4].
    ldr     w10, [x9, #0]                   // m_source (signed Endpoint)
    sxtw    x10, w10
    mov     x0, x10
    mov     x1, #SEND
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_b_end
_user_stub_b_end:
    .size _user_stub_b_start, _user_stub_b_end - _user_stub_b_start

/* ---- Stub C: kernel-call client --------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_c, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_c_start
    .type _user_stub_c_start, %object
_user_stub_c_start:
    mov     x19, #0                         // persistent call counter
1:
    // Build the request at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0):
    //   [+0]   m_source — kernel overwrites
    //   [+4]   m_type    = SYS_GETINFO
    //   [+8]   payload[0..4] = GET_WHOAMI
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]
    mov     w10, #SYS_GETINFO
    str     w10, [x9, #4]
    mov     w10, #GET_WHOAMI
    str     w10, [x9, #8]
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_SYS
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_c_end
_user_stub_c_end:
    .size _user_stub_c_start, _user_stub_c_end - _user_stub_c_start

/* ---- Stub D: page-fault demo (slice 3.4b) ------------------------------ */

/*
 * Stub D touches an unmapped heap page at 0x0100_0000 in a loop. The first
 * store faults (translation); the kernel records the fault, blocks D on
 * RTS_PAGEFAULT, and sends VM_PAGEFAULT to the user-space VM server. VM maps
 * the page (SYS_VMCTL(VMCTL_PT_MAP)) and clears the fault
 * (VMCTL_CLEAR_PAGEFAULT); D's store then retries and succeeds. Every later
 * iteration writes the now-mapped page with no fault. D issues no SVC.
 */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_d, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_d_start
    .type _user_stub_d_start, %object
_user_stub_d_start:
    movz    x20, #0x0100, lsl #16              // x20 = 0x0100_0000 (heap VA)
    mov     x19, #0                            // persistent counter
1:
    str     x19, [x20]                         // 1st iter faults → kernel→VM maps it
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_d_end
_user_stub_d_end:
    .size _user_stub_d_start, _user_stub_d_end - _user_stub_d_start

input.new_string:
- **Slice 3.5** ◀ ready (branch `feature/phase-3-5-vm-brk`, pending merge) — VM
  region tracking + `VM_BRK`. New `servers/vm/src/region.rs`: a static
  `[ClientRegions; 16]` table (`UnsafeCell` newtype, keyed by proc number;
  `MAX_REGIONS = 4`) with `HEAP_BASE = 0x0100_0000`, `set_brk` (find-or-create
  the Heap region, page-align the new break) and `contains` (region lookup for
  the fault path). The VM server's receive loop dispatches `VM_BRK` to a
  `handle_brk` that replies to the SENDREC caller with the resulting break, and
  `handle_pagefault` now gates `VMCTL_PT_MAP` on `region::contains` — faults
  outside every region take a silent SIGSEGV path (faulter left blocked on
  `RTS_PAGEFAULT`; real signals are Phase 4). `kernel-shared/callnr.rs` gains
  `VM_BRK = VM_RQ_BASE + 1` (`0xC01`) + a host test. Stub D rewritten from a
  fault-on-touch blob into a brk client: `VM_BRK(0x0100_4000)` → touch page 0 →
  `VM_BRK(0x0100_8000)` → touch page 1 (only in range after the grow) → loop;
  `install_stub_d_priv` widened from `trap_mask = TSK_T` to `USR_T` with
  `ipc_to` opened to VM, and VM's `ipc_to` opened back to D (its priv slot 19 is
  past the `[0, n_active)` boot fill, so VM couldn't otherwise reply). Verified
  in QEMU: `[pf] proc=D far=0x1000000` + `far=0x1004000`, two
  `[ksys VMCTL_PT_MAP] proc=D va=0x1000000`/`va=0x1004000` `result=0`, then D
  round-robins with no re-fault; A↔B + C intact, no panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`.
- **Slice 3.6** ◀ next — `VM_MMAP` / `VM_MUNMAP` + Phase 3 doc/CLAUDE.md cleanup.

+  the Heap region, page-align the new break) and `contains` (region lookup for
+  the fault path). The VM server's receive loop dispatches `VM_BRK` to a
+  `handle_brk` that replies to the SENDREC caller with the resulting break, and
+  outside every region take a silent SIGSEGV path (faulter left blocked on
+  `VM_BRK = VM_RQ_BASE + 1` (`0xC01`) + a host test. Stub D rewritten from a
+  fault-on-touch blob into a brk client: `VM_BRK(0x0100_4000)` → touch page 0 →
+  `VM_BRK(0x0100_8000)` → touch page 1 (only in range after the grow) → loop;
+  `ipc_to` opened to VM, and VM's `ipc_to` opened back to D (its priv slot 19 is
+  past the `[0, n_active)` boot fill, so VM couldn't otherwise reply). Verified
+  round-robins with no re-fault; A↔B + C intact, no panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`.

content.text:
Slice 3.5 is implemented and verified. Here's the summary.

## Slice 3.5 — VM region tracking + `VM_BRK` ✅

**What changed** (6 files):

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs` | `VM_BRK = VM_RQ_BASE + 1` (`0xC01`) + host test |
| `servers/vm/src/region.rs` *(new)* | Static `[ClientRegions; 16]` table (`UnsafeCell` newtype, keyed by proc number, `HEAP_BASE = 0x0100_0000`); `set_brk` (find-or-create heap region, page-align break) + `contains` |
| `servers/vm/src/main.rs` | Receive loop dispatches `VM_BRK` → `handle_brk` (replies to SENDREC caller); `handle_pagefault` now gates `VMCTL_PT_MAP` on `region::contains`, with a silent SIGSEGV path for out-of-region faults |
| `kernel/.../userland.rs` | `install_stub_d_priv` widened `TSK_T`→`USR_T` + `ipc_to`→VM; **also opens VM's `ipc_to`→D** (D's priv slot 19 is past the `[0, n_active)` boot fill, so VM couldn't otherwise reply) |
| `kernel/.../user_stub.S` | Stub D rewritten: `VM_BRK(0x1004000)` → touch page 0 → `VM_BRK(0x1008000)` → touch page 1 → loop |
| `docs/plan.md` | 3.4b → shipped (PR #14), 3.5 → ready, 3.6 → next |

**The key finding from exploration** that the decomposition doc didn't flag: VM's `ipc_to` is filled only for boot priv slots ≈0–15, so it could not reply to stub D (slot 19) — slice 3.5 had to wire that direction explicitly.

**Verification** (`/tmp/phase35.log`, 8s QEMU boot):
- `[pf] proc=D far=0x1000000` **and** `far=0x1004000` — both heap pages fault.
- `[ksys VMCTL_PT_MAP] proc=D va=0x1000000 … result=0` and `va=0x1004000 … result=0` — both mapped on demand. The second map only happens because `VM_BRK(0x1008000)` grew the region to include page 1; without the grow it would have been an out-of-region SIGSEGV.
- Exactly **2 faults, no re-faults**; D round-robins to 93% of the run (68 ticks).
- Regressions intact: A↔B ping-pong (`[ipc]`), C `SYS_GETINFO` (`[ksys]` `caller=13 call=0`).
- **Zero** panics, `el0_sync_unexpected`, SIGSEGV, or nonzero `result=`.
- `cargo test -p minix4-kernel-shared`: 29 passed; `cargo check --workspace` and `cargo kernel-aarch64` clean, no warnings.

Changes are on branch `feature/phase-3-5-vm-brk`, not committed. Per your workflow I'll hold for your review — want me to commit, and run the pre-PR CLAUDE.md check before opening the PR?

content.text:
Review this session for learnings about working with Claude Code in this codebase. Update CLAUDE.md with context that would help future Claude sessions be more effective.

## Step 1: Reflect

What context was missing that would have helped Claude work more effectively?
- Bash commands that were used or discovered
- Code style patterns followed
- Testing approaches that worked
- Environment/configuration quirks
- Warnings or gotchas encountered

## Step 2: Find CLAUDE.md Files

```bash
find . -name "CLAUDE.md" -o -name ".claude.local.md" 2>/dev/null | head -20
```

Decide where each addition belongs:
- `CLAUDE.md` - Team-shared (checked into git)
- `.claude.local.md` - Personal/local only (gitignored)

## Step 3: Draft Additions

**Keep it concise** - one line per concept. CLAUDE.md is part of the prompt, so brevity matters.

Format: `<command or pattern>` - `<brief description>`

Avoid:
- Verbose explanations
- Obvious information
- One-off fixes unlikely to recur

## Step 4: Show Proposed Changes

For each addition:

```
### Update: ./CLAUDE.md

**Why:** [one-line reason]

\`\`\`diff
+ [the addition - keep it brief]
\`\`\`
```

## Step 5: Apply with Approval

Ask if the user wants to apply the changes. Only edit files they approve.

content.content: ./CLAUDE.md

content.text:
**Reflection** — two non-obvious gotchas surfaced this session that future server-slice work will hit again:

1. **User-space servers can't print** — VM runs at EL0 with no console facility yet, so server behavior is only observable via kernel-side traces. I had to revise my plan after assuming VM could emit `[vm] …` lines.
2. **`ipc_to` reverse-wiring for high priv slots** — `init_boot_image` fills a server's `ipc_to` only for boot priv slots `[0, n_active)` (~0–15); the hand-installed stubs (slots 16+) need the server→stub direction opened explicitly or the server's reply is denied.

Everything else (UnsafeCell-newtype tables, gated ELF attrs, build/embed flow, QEMU verification rhythm) is already documented.

### Update: ./CLAUDE.md

**Why:** Every future server slice (PM, VFS, …) will debug behavior with no server-side print; knowing to look at kernel traces saves a wrong turn.

```diff
+ - User-space servers run at EL0 with no console access — they cannot print. Verify server behavior through kernel-side traces (`[pf]` from `do_page_fault`, `[ksys …]` from `do_vmctl`/`system`, `[ipc N]` head-trace from `ipc::dispatch`), never server-side logging
```

**Why:** Reply-permission denials from this are silent-ish (`ECALLDENIED`/`EBADSRCDST` on the trace) and easy to miss when wiring a new stub↔server pair.

```diff
+ - `init_boot_image` fills a boot server's `ipc_to` only for active boot priv slots `[0, n_active)` (~0–15). A hand-installed stub in a higher priv slot (16+) that a server must *reply* to needs the reverse `ipc_to` bit opened explicitly — see `install_stub_d_priv` opening VM→D after setting D→VM
```

Both go in the **Code Conventions** bullet list. Want me to apply these two additions?

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

content.text: I'll add both bullets to the Code Conventions section.

content.content: <system-reminder>Warning: the file exists but is shorter than the provided offset (98). The file has 98 lines.</system-reminder>

70	| `fs/*` | File system servers (MFS, PFS) |
71	| `userland/*` | User programs (init, sh, coreutils) |
72	
73	## Code Conventions
74	
75	- The kernel ships `--release` only, so `debug_assert!` is compiled out. Use a hard `assert!` for invariants whose violation would silently corrupt (e.g. a null TTBR0/ASID reaching the scheduler); reserve `debug_assert!` for cheap "can't happen" documentation that's fine to drop in release
76	- Kernel `unsafe` blocks require `// SAFETY:` comments documenting the invariant
77	- IPC linked lists use `Option<ProcNr>` indices into static arrays, not raw pointers
78	- Message types are defined in `kernel-shared` and shared across all crates
79	- Assembly is confined to `.S` files (assembled via `cc` crate in `build.rs`); use `core::arch::asm!` only for single-instruction operations
80	- New `.S` files must be added to `kernel/build.rs`'s `sources` array; offset blocks (`.equ REGS_*_OFFSET …`) are duplicated per-file since there is no cross-`.S` include
81	- To end a `&mut` borrow before an `unsafe` call that re-borrows the same static, capture state into locals (bool / scalar) and rely on NLL — `drop(&mut x)` is a no-op and triggers a `dropping_references` warning
82	- Run-queue admission is decoupled from boot: `IMAGE.runnable` marks IPC reachability; only `proc::sched::enqueue` puts a proc in the scheduler's run queue
83	- Static mutable tables use `UnsafeCell<[T; N]>` inside a `#[repr(transparent)]` newtype with `unsafe impl Sync`; document the single-threaded-boot invariant in the `// SAFETY:` comment
84	- Custom `Display` impls that must honor `{:<width$}` render through a stack buffer (`arrayvec::ArrayString<N>`) and call `f.pad(s)` — `write!(f, ...)` from inside `Display::fmt` ignores the outer width spec
85	- Forward declarations intended for later slices (constants, fields, re-exports) get module-level `#![allow(dead_code)]` with a one-line comment naming the consuming slice
86	- IPC primitives take an explicit `&mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS]` (and `&mut [Priv; NR_SYS_PROCS]`) slice; only `ipc::do_ipc` materializes those from `PROC_TABLE` / `PRIV_TABLE` via `proc_table_mut_slice` / `priv_table_mut_slice`. Keeps each primitive testable in isolation and dodges the two-`&mut`-from-one-`UnsafeCell` UB hazard
87	- Every EL1 → EL0 transition (SVC tail via `el1_svc_tail`, `sched::reschedule`, `sched::run`) calls `sched::schedule_next`, which flushes `Proc::deliver_msg` to the user buffer at `Proc::deliver_msg_vir` and clears `MF_DELIVERMSG` before resuming
88	- IPC blocking pairs with the new `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset` helpers — they capture `nr`, end the `&mut Proc` borrow, then call `enqueue` / `dequeue` so RTS state and the run queue stay in sync. Same NLL-capture pattern slice 2.4 used in `clock::tick`
89	- Kernel-call handlers that act on a *target* proc named in the message (e.g. `system::do_vmctl`) take the whole `&mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS]` slice + `caller_nr`; caller-only handlers (e.g. `do_getinfo`) get a single `&mut Proc` / `&Priv`. `system::kernel_call_dispatch` routes `SYS_VMCTL` to the table-taking form and the rest through `dispatch_caller_local`. Run-queue transitions on a target use the same `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset` capture-then-borrow-end pattern the IPC primitives use
90	- `kernel/build.rs` skips assembly when `CARGO_CFG_TARGET_OS != "none"` so `cargo check --workspace` / `cargo test --workspace` keep working on host. The kernel's real modules are gated by `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` in `main.rs` regardless
91	- `cargo test -p minix4-kernel` runs zero tests by design — every kernel module is gated on `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` and host-test infra is not yet built; host-runnable tests live in `kernel-shared`. QEMU is the primary verification for kernel code (`timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release`)
92	- User-space servers build as freestanding `#![no_std]`/`#![no_main]` ELFs linked with their own `user.ld` (page-aligned PT_LOADs, base `0x10_0000`; kernel sets `sp_el0` so `_start` needs no stack setup). The kernel's `build.rs` builds them for `aarch64-unknown-none` in a *separate* `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (dodges the nested-cargo build-lock deadlock), overrides the kernel linker script via `CARGO_ENCODED_RUSTFLAGS` → the server's `user.ld`, and emits `VM_ELF_PATH` so `kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs` can `include_bytes!` it. `boot_image/elf.rs` is the minimal ET_EXEC/AArch64 loader (PT_LOAD → `alloc_frame` + HHDM copy + map; BSS via zeroed frames). The multi-module MXBI archive is deferred until Phase 4 loads more than one server
93	- ELF-only attributes on server crates (`#[unsafe(link_section = ".text._start")]`, etc.) must be `#[cfg_attr(target_os = "none", ...)]`-gated — `cargo check --workspace` also builds servers for the Mach-O host, which rejects ELF section specifiers
94	
95	## Documentation

Flip the previous slice forward and slide `◀ next` ahead as part of each slice's PR. When opening a new slice PR, also reconcile any older `◀ ready` markers against `git log` — stale "pending merge" labels on already-merged PRs accumulate otherwise.

## Code Conventions

- The kernel ships `--release` only, so `debug_assert!` is compiled out. Use a hard `assert!` for invariants whose violation would silently corrupt (e.g. a null TTBR0/ASID reaching the scheduler); reserve `debug_assert!` for cheap "can't happen" documentation that's fine to drop in release
- Kernel `unsafe` blocks require `// SAFETY:` comments documenting the invariant
- IPC linked lists use `Option<ProcNr>` indices into static arrays, not raw pointers
- Message types are defined in `kernel-shared` and shared across all crates
- Assembly is confined to `.S` files (assembled via `cc` crate in `build.rs`); use `core::arch::asm!` only for single-instruction operations
- New `.S` files must be added to `kernel/build.rs`'s `sources` array; offset blocks (`.equ REGS_*_OFFSET …`) are duplicated per-file since there is no cross-`.S` include
- To end a `&mut` borrow before an `unsafe` call that re-borrows the same static, capture state into locals (bool / scalar) and rely on NLL — `drop(&mut x)` is a no-op and triggers a `dropping_references` warning
- Run-queue admission is decoupled from boot: `IMAGE.runnable` marks IPC reachability; only `proc::sched::enqueue` puts a proc in the scheduler's run queue
- Static mutable tables use `UnsafeCell<[T; N]>` inside a `#[repr(transparent)]` newtype with `unsafe impl Sync`; document the single-threaded-boot invariant in the `// SAFETY:` comment
- Custom `Display` impls that must honor `{:<width$}` render through a stack buffer (`arrayvec::ArrayString<N>`) and call `f.pad(s)` — `write!(f, ...)` from inside `Display::fmt` ignores the outer width spec
- Forward declarations intended for later slices (constants, fields, re-exports) get module-level `#![allow(dead_code)]` with a one-line comment naming the consuming slice
- IPC primitives take an explicit `&mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS]` (and `&mut [Priv; NR_SYS_PROCS]`) slice; only `ipc::do_ipc` materializes those from `PROC_TABLE` / `PRIV_TABLE` via `proc_table_mut_slice` / `priv_table_mut_slice`. Keeps each primitive testable in isolation and dodges the two-`&mut`-from-one-`UnsafeCell` UB hazard
- Every EL1 → EL0 transition (SVC tail via `el1_svc_tail`, `sched::reschedule`, `sched::run`) calls `sched::schedule_next`, which flushes `Proc::deliver_msg` to the user buffer at `Proc::deliver_msg_vir` and clears `MF_DELIVERMSG` before resuming
- IPC blocking pairs with the new `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset` helpers — they capture `nr`, end the `&mut Proc` borrow, then call `enqueue` / `dequeue` so RTS state and the run queue stay in sync. Same NLL-capture pattern slice 2.4 used in `clock::tick`
- Kernel-call handlers that act on a *target* proc named in the message (e.g. `system::do_vmctl`) take the whole `&mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS]` slice + `caller_nr`; caller-only handlers (e.g. `do_getinfo`) get a single `&mut Proc` / `&Priv`. `system::kernel_call_dispatch` routes `SYS_VMCTL` to the table-taking form and the rest through `dispatch_caller_local`. Run-queue transitions on a target use the same `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset` capture-then-borrow-end pattern the IPC primitives use
- `kernel/build.rs` skips assembly when `CARGO_CFG_TARGET_OS != "none"` so `cargo check --workspace` / `cargo test --workspace` keep working on host. The kernel's real modules are gated by `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` in `main.rs` regardless
- `cargo test -p minix4-kernel` runs zero tests by design — every kernel module is gated on `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` and host-test infra is not yet built; host-runnable tests live in `kernel-shared`. QEMU is the primary verification for kernel code (`timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release`)
- User-space servers build as freestanding `#![no_std]`/`#![no_main]` ELFs linked with their own `user.ld` (page-aligned PT_LOADs, base `0x10_0000`; kernel sets `sp_el0` so `_start` needs no stack setup). The kernel's `build.rs` builds them for `aarch64-unknown-none` in a *separate* `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (dodges the nested-cargo build-lock deadlock), overrides the kernel linker script via `CARGO_ENCODED_RUSTFLAGS` → the server's `user.ld`, and emits `VM_ELF_PATH` so `kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs` can `include_bytes!` it. `boot_image/elf.rs` is the minimal ET_EXEC/AArch64 loader (PT_LOAD → `alloc_frame` + HHDM copy + map; BSS via zeroed frames). The multi-module MXBI archive is deferred until Phase 4 loads more than one server
- ELF-only attributes on server crates (`#[unsafe(link_section = ".text._start")]`, etc.) must be `#[cfg_attr(target_os = "none", ...)]`-gated — `cargo check --workspace` also builds servers for the Mach-O host, which rejects ELF section specifiers

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# MINIX 4

MINIX 4 is a learning OS with a Rust microkernel, based on MINIX 3's architecture.

## Project Overview

- **Kernel:** Rust (no_std, no_main), greenfield microkernel
- **Architecture:** aarch64 primary (Apple Silicon / QEMU virt), x86_64 secondary
- **Servers:** PM, VFS, VM, RS, DS, SCHED as user-space Rust processes
- **Drivers:** VirtIO (MMIO for aarch64, PCI for x86_64) as user-space processes
- **C library:** musl-libc fork with MINIX IPC syscall wrappers
- **License:** BSD-2-Clause only (no GPL code)
- **Platform:** QEMU as primary target

## Reference Codebase

MINIX 4's architecture is based on MINIX 3. When the docs reference "MINIX 3 source",
they mean paths within the MINIX 3 source tree (e.g., `kernel/proc.c` means the
`kernel/proc.c` file in a MINIX 3 checkout). Key reference files:

- `kernel/proc.c` -- IPC implementation (mini_send, mini_receive, deadlock detection)
- `kernel/proc.h`, `priv.h` -- Process and privilege structures
- `kernel/system.c` -- Kernel call dispatch
- `include/minix/ipc.h` -- Message structure definitions
- `include/minix/com.h`, `callnr.h` -- Server endpoints, call numbers
- `lib/libc/sys/*.c` -- POSIX syscall wrappers (template for musl adaptation)
- `lib/libsys/sef.c` -- SEF framework (template for server-rt)

The MINIX 3 source is available at https://github.com/Stichting-MINIX-Research-Foundation/minix

## Build

```sh
# Build kernel for aarch64 (primary target)
cargo kernel-aarch64

# Boot in QEMU (cargo runner wires tools/qemu-run.sh). The kernel runs
# indefinitely once EL0 starts (slice 2.4+), so `timeout` is mandatory.
# Redirect to a file when you need to grep tick output -- live tail loses lines.
# The log interleaves raw single-char tick bytes; grep it with `grep -a`
# (force text) or matches read as "Binary file matches".
timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release

# Build kernel for x86_64
cargo kernel-x86_64

# Run host-side unit tests (note the package name, not the dir name)
cargo test -p minix4-kernel-shared
```

## Architecture

See `docs/architecture.md` for the full system design. Key concepts:

- **Microkernel:** Only IPC, scheduling, interrupt dispatch, and memory protection in kernel
- **Message passing:** 6 IPC primitives (SEND, RECEIVE, SENDREC, NOTIFY, SENDNB, SENDA)
- **User-space servers:** All OS services (file system, process management, memory management) run as separate processes communicating via IPC
- **Privilege model:** Fine-grained bitmaps control which processes can communicate and what kernel calls they can make

## Workspace Crates

| Crate | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `kernel` | Microkernel (no_std) |
| `kernel-shared` | Message types, endpoints, call numbers shared between kernel and userspace |
| `minix-ipc` | User-space IPC library (SVC/SYSCALL asm stubs) |
| `server-rt` | Server runtime / SEF framework |
| `servers/*` | System servers (PM, VFS, VM, RS, DS, SCHED) |
| `drivers/*` | Device drivers (VirtIO block/net/console, memory) |
| `fs/*` | File system servers (MFS, PFS) |
| `userland/*` | User programs (init, sh, coreutils) |

## Code Conventions

- The kernel ships `--release` only, so `debug_assert!` is compiled out. Use a hard `assert!` for invariants whose violation would silently corrupt (e.g. a null TTBR0/ASID reaching the scheduler); reserve `debug_assert!` for cheap "can't happen" documentation that's fine to drop in release
- Kernel `unsafe` blocks require `// SAFETY:` comments documenting the invariant
- IPC linked lists use `Option<ProcNr>` indices into static arrays, not raw pointers
- Message types are defined in `kernel-shared` and shared across all crates
- Assembly is confined to `.S` files (assembled via `cc` crate in `build.rs`); use `core::arch::asm!` only for single-instruction operations
- New `.S` files must be added to `kernel/build.rs`'s `sources` array; offset blocks (`.equ REGS_*_OFFSET …`) are duplicated per-file since there is no cross-`.S` include
- To end a `&mut` borrow before an `unsafe` call that re-borrows the same static, capture state into locals (bool / scalar) and rely on NLL — `drop(&mut x)` is a no-op and triggers a `dropping_references` warning
- Run-queue admission is decoupled from boot: `IMAGE.runnable` marks IPC reachability; only `proc::sched::enqueue` puts a proc in the scheduler's run queue
- Static mutable tables use `UnsafeCell<[T; N]>` inside a `#[repr(transparent)]` newtype with `unsafe impl Sync`; document the single-threaded-boot invariant in the `// SAFETY:` comment
- Custom `Display` impls that must honor `{:<width$}` render through a stack buffer (`arrayvec::ArrayString<N>`) and call `f.pad(s)` — `write!(f, ...)` from inside `Display::fmt` ignores the outer width spec
- Forward declarations intended for later slices (constants, fields, re-exports) get module-level `#![allow(dead_code)]` with a one-line comment naming the consuming slice
- IPC primitives take an explicit `&mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS]` (and `&mut [Priv; NR_SYS_PROCS]`) slice; only `ipc::do_ipc` materializes those from `PROC_TABLE` / `PRIV_TABLE` via `proc_table_mut_slice` / `priv_table_mut_slice`. Keeps each primitive testable in isolation and dodges the two-`&mut`-from-one-`UnsafeCell` UB hazard
- Every EL1 → EL0 transition (SVC tail via `el1_svc_tail`, `sched::reschedule`, `sched::run`) calls `sched::schedule_next`, which flushes `Proc::deliver_msg` to the user buffer at `Proc::deliver_msg_vir` and clears `MF_DELIVERMSG` before resuming
- IPC blocking pairs with the new `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset` helpers — they capture `nr`, end the `&mut Proc` borrow, then call `enqueue` / `dequeue` so RTS state and the run queue stay in sync. Same NLL-capture pattern slice 2.4 used in `clock::tick`
- Kernel-call handlers that act on a *target* proc named in the message (e.g. `system::do_vmctl`) take the whole `&mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS]` slice + `caller_nr`; caller-only handlers (e.g. `do_getinfo`) get a single `&mut Proc` / `&Priv`. `system::kernel_call_dispatch` routes `SYS_VMCTL` to the table-taking form and the rest through `dispatch_caller_local`. Run-queue transitions on a target use the same `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset` capture-then-borrow-end pattern the IPC primitives use
- `kernel/build.rs` skips assembly when `CARGO_CFG_TARGET_OS != "none"` so `cargo check --workspace` / `cargo test --workspace` keep working on host. The kernel's real modules are gated by `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` in `main.rs` regardless
- `cargo test -p minix4-kernel` runs zero tests by design — every kernel module is gated on `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` and host-test infra is not yet built; host-runnable tests live in `kernel-shared`. QEMU is the primary verification for kernel code (`timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release`)
- User-space servers build as freestanding `#![no_std]`/`#![no_main]` ELFs linked with their own `user.ld` (page-aligned PT_LOADs, base `0x10_0000`; kernel sets `sp_el0` so `_start` needs no stack setup). The kernel's `build.rs` builds them for `aarch64-unknown-none` in a *separate* `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (dodges the nested-cargo build-lock deadlock), overrides the kernel linker script via `CARGO_ENCODED_RUSTFLAGS` → the server's `user.ld`, and emits `VM_ELF_PATH` so `kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs` can `include_bytes!` it. `boot_image/elf.rs` is the minimal ET_EXEC/AArch64 loader (PT_LOAD → `alloc_frame` + HHDM copy + map; BSS via zeroed frames). The multi-module MXBI archive is deferred until Phase 4 loads more than one server
- ELF-only attributes on server crates (`#[unsafe(link_section = ".text._start")]`, etc.) must be `#[cfg_attr(target_os = "none", ...)]`-gated — `cargo check --workspace` also builds servers for the Mach-O host, which rejects ELF section specifiers

## Documentation

`docs/plan.md` tracks slice status with three markers: `◀ next` (unstarted), `◀ ready (branch ..., pending merge)` (implemented but unmerged), `✓ shipped (PR #N, merged YYYY-MM-DD)` (merged). Flip the previous slice forward and slide `◀ next` ahead as part of each slice's PR. When opening a new slice PR, also reconcile any older `◀ ready` markers against `git log` — stale "pending merge" labels on already-merged PRs accumulate otherwise.

QEMU is the primary verification for kernel code (`timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release`)

- User-space servers build as freestanding `#![no_std]`/`#![no_main]` ELFs linked with their own `user.ld` (page-aligned PT_LOADs, base `0x10_0000`; kernel sets `sp_el0` so `_start` needs no stack setup). The kernel's `build.rs` builds them for `aarch64-unknown-none` in a *separate* `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (dodges the nested-cargo build-lock deadlock), overrides the kernel linker script via `CARGO_ENCODED_RUSTFLAGS` → the server's `user.ld`, and emits `VM_ELF_PATH` so `kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs` can `include_bytes!` it. `boot_image/elf.rs` is the minimal ET_EXEC/AArch64 loader (PT_LOAD → `alloc_frame` + HHDM copy + map; BSS via zeroed frames). The multi-module MXBI archive is deferred until Phase 4 loads more than one server

- ELF-only attributes on server crates (`#[unsafe(link_section = ".text._start")]`, etc.) must be `#[cfg_attr(target_os = "none", ...)]`-gated — `cargo check --workspace` also builds servers for the Mach-O host, which rejects ELF section specifiers

content.text: Now stage, commit, and push. Let me check sandbox/signing state by attempting the commit:

input.command:
cd /Users/kevinbarnard/src/minix-4
git add -A
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
phase 3.5: VM region tracking + VM_BRK

Give the VM server per-process memory state and a brk request so a process
can grow its heap, replacing slice 3.4b's "map any fault" behavior.

- kernel-shared: add VM_BRK = VM_RQ_BASE + 1 (0xC01) + host test
- servers/vm/region.rs: static [ClientRegions; 16] table (UnsafeCell newtype,
  keyed by proc number, HEAP_BASE = 0x0100_0000); set_brk find-or-creates the
  heap region (page-aligned break), contains() gates the fault path
- servers/vm/main.rs: dispatch VM_BRK -> handle_brk (replies to the SENDREC
  caller); handle_pagefault now satisfies a fault only when region::contains,
  else a silent SIGSEGV (faulter left blocked; real signals are Phase 4)
- userland.rs: stub D widened TSK_T -> USR_T with ipc_to -> VM, and VM's
  ipc_to opened back to D (its priv slot 19 is past init_boot_image's
  [0, n_active) fill, so VM otherwise can't reply)
- user_stub.S: stub D rewritten as a brk client — VM_BRK(0x1004000), touch
  page 0, VM_BRK(0x1008000), touch page 1 (only in range after the grow), loop
- docs/plan.md: 3.4b -> shipped (PR #14), 3.5 -> ready, 3.6 -> next
- CLAUDE.md: pin EL0-servers-can't-print and ipc_to reverse-wiring gotchas

Verified in QEMU (8s): [pf] proc=D far=0x1000000 + far=0x1004000, two
[ksys VMCTL_PT_MAP] proc=D va=0x1000000/va=0x1004000 result=0, then D
round-robins with no re-fault; A<->B + C intact; no panic, no
el0_sync_unexpected, no nonzero results. kernel-shared host tests 29 pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -15

input.command:
cd /Users/kevinbarnard/src/minix-4
gh pr create --base main --head feature/phase-3-5-vm-brk --title "phase 3.5: VM region tracking + VM_BRK" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Slice 3.5 — VM region tracking + `VM_BRK`

Gives the user-space VM server per-process memory state and a `brk` request so a process can grow its heap, replacing slice 3.4b's "satisfy any fault" behavior. Faults are now resolved only when the address lies inside a known region; out-of-region faults take a (silent, pre-PM) SIGSEGV path.

### Changes
- **`kernel-shared/callnr.rs`**: `VM_BRK = VM_RQ_BASE + 1` (`0xC01`) + host test.
- **`servers/vm/src/region.rs`** *(new)*: static `[ClientRegions; 16]` table (`UnsafeCell` newtype, keyed by proc number, `MAX_REGIONS = 4`, `HEAP_BASE = 0x0100_0000`). `set_brk` find-or-creates the heap region with a page-aligned break; `contains` gates the fault path.
- **`servers/vm/src/main.rs`**: receive loop dispatches `VM_BRK` → `handle_brk` (replies to the SENDREC caller with the resulting break); `handle_pagefault` now satisfies a fault only when `region::contains`, else returns without mapping/clearing (faulter stays blocked — real signals are Phase 4).
- **`kernel/.../userland.rs`**: `install_stub_d_priv` widened `TSK_T` → `USR_T` with `ipc_to` → VM, **and** opens VM's `ipc_to` back to D — D's priv slot (19) is past `init_boot_image`'s `[0, n_active)` fill, so VM otherwise couldn't reply.
- **`kernel/.../user_stub.S`**: stub D rewritten from fault-on-touch into a brk client — `VM_BRK(0x1004000)` → touch page 0 → `VM_BRK(0x1008000)` → touch page 1 (only in range after the grow) → loop.
- **`docs/plan.md`**: 3.4b → shipped (PR #14), 3.5 → ready, 3.6 → next.
- **`CLAUDE.md`**: pin two gotchas (EL0 servers can't print → verify via kernel traces; `ipc_to` reverse-wiring for high priv slots).

### Verification (QEMU, 8s `aarch64-unknown-none --release`)
- `[pf] proc=D far=0x1000000` **and** `far=0x1004000` — both heap pages fault.
- `[ksys VMCTL_PT_MAP] proc=D va=0x1000000 … result=0` and `va=0x1004000 … result=0` — both mapped on demand. The second only happens because `VM_BRK(0x1008000)` grew the region to include page 1; without it that fault would be an out-of-region SIGSEGV.
- Exactly **2 faults, no re-faults**; D round-robins to 93% of the run.
- Regressions intact: A↔B ping-pong (`[ipc]`), C `SYS_GETINFO` (`[ksys] caller=13 call=0`).
- **Zero** panics, `el0_sync_unexpected`, SIGSEGV, or nonzero `result=`.
- `cargo test -p minix4-kernel-shared`: 29 passed; `cargo check --workspace` + `cargo kernel-aarch64` clean, no warnings.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

content.text:
Done. **PR #15** is open: https://github.com/KevinBarnard/minix-4/pull/15

## Summary

Slice 3.5 (VM region tracking + `VM_BRK`) is committed (GPG-signed, `cc37a64`), pushed, and PR'd against `main`.

- **7 files**, +349/−51, including the new `servers/vm/src/region.rs`.
- Commit and PR body carry the full verification evidence; QEMU trace confirms both heap pages map on demand after their respective `brk` calls, with no re-faults and all regressions (A↔B ping-pong, C `SYS_GETINFO`) intact.
- Pre-PR checklist honored: `CLAUDE.md` updated with two session learnings (EL0 servers can't print → verify via kernel traces; `ipc_to` reverse-wiring for priv slots past the boot fill).

Per your global workflow, after this merges on GitHub (regular merge, not squash): `git checkout main && git pull`, delete the local + remote `feature/phase-3-5-vm-brk` branch, and `git fetch --prune`. Next up is **Slice 3.6** — `VM_MMAP`/`VM_MUNMAP` + Phase 3 doc/CLAUDE.md cleanup.
